title: Anno Hideakis Evangelion Interview with Azuma Hiroki
display_title: Anno Hideakis Evangelion Interview with Azuma Hiroki
wikitext:
[]: Anno Hideaki's Evangelion: Interview with Azuma Hiroki             Quote Post by LiLi » Sat Aug 29, 2009 8:26 am First time creating a topic, I hope I'm doing this right... I found this article: TOWARDS A CARTOGRAPHY OF Japanese ANIME Anno Hideaki's Evangelion Interview with Azuma Hiroki by Krystian Woznicki for BLIMP Filmmagazine http://www.nettime.org/Lists-Archives/nettime-l-9802/msg00101.html I thought it was worth sharing, at least for the interview part. In particular, because it also addresses certain topics that came up in other threads on this forum such as Rei Ayanami's apartment and the relation between Eva/Anno and Aum. I also think the references to Japanese culture and society provide an interesting lens through which to look at Director Anno's work.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Sat Aug 29, 2009 11:11 am When I first read about Aum Shinrikyo in a book about terrorism a year ago, I noticed a similarity between Eva and Aum beliefs. I never knew until now that there was a relation between the two. Very interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Aug 29, 2009 11:27 am I think I read this before but it's a good read eitherway. Thanks!

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Aug 29, 2009 11:28 am Yeah, I also read this a while ago. Azuma wrote that great "Anime or Something Like it" article and, IIRC, that has a lot of the same content.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Sat Aug 29, 2009 2:56 pm Holy Diver wrote: When I first read about Aum Shinrikyo in a book about terrorism a year ago, I noticed a similarity between Eva and Aum beliefs. I never knew until now that there was a relation between the two. Very interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Aug 30, 2009 1:47 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Yeah, I also read this a while ago. Azuma wrote that great "Anime or Something Like it" article and, IIRC, that has a lot of the same content.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Sun Aug 30, 2009 2:20 pm ^ I SCOWL at your greediness Xard!

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Aug 30, 2009 2:49 pm Xard wrote: I was the one who gave you link to this article

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Aug 31, 2009 12:22 pm LiLi wrote: ^ I SCOWL at your greediness Xard!

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Mon Aug 31, 2009 12:30 pm Xard wrote: LiLi wrote: ^ I SCOWL at your greediness Xard!

[]:             Quote Post by sean_sean_sean_sean » Mon Aug 31, 2009 12:34 pm I just found something entirely new - BBC interview of Anno about Love&Pop nnya nnya nnyaa

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Mon Aug 31, 2009 12:39 pm ^Actually, the BBC interview sounds familiar... I wonder if I have already seen it somewhere... Anyway: any thoughts on the article above, anyone?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 31, 2009 1:50 pm Xard wrote: You have a leaky memory do you?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Aug 31, 2009 1:59 pm sean_sean_sean_sean wrote: I just found something entirely new - BBC interview of Anno about Love&Pop nnya nnya nnyaa

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 31, 2009 2:05 pm What did smarty-pants Xardy-poo figure out?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Aug 31, 2009 2:15 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: What did smarty-pants Xardy-poo figure out?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 31, 2009 3:40 pm Xard wrote: not TELLING YOU! after that :'(

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Aug 31, 2009 4:39 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: (In a totally straight and manly way, of course)

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Sep 02, 2009 2:28 pm no commentsu?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Sep 02, 2009 8:20 pm Oops! I lost track of this thread. I'll read and get back to you soon. I've got a lot going on right now and I'm online for a few minutes. Edit: Well, I read it... Hmmm... It's quite interesting. The disorienting (I think that was the word you were looking for) camerawork is certainly one of the most outstanding features and it definitely makes sense that it provides such a contrast with the "normality" of these girls' lives. I might have more substance to add later. BTW, I tested the Shiki-Jitsu DVD and it works fine. It doesn't have *the best* picture quality, but with my awesomesauce DVD player I can tweak the image to make it quite a bit better and cut down on some of the grain and visual noise. I should be able to watch it soon (I also have those 3 Mizoguchi films).
